question: Which pieces from Swiss Artists are in the Musée d'art et d'histoire in Geneva?
reference_answer: There are 338 pieces from 96 Swiss artists in the museum. Swiss artists are identified via two criteria: (1) born in a place that falls within Switzerland (P92i_was_brought_into_existence_by → P7_took_place_at → P89_falls_within* Switzerland), or (2) member of a group with 'suisse' in label (P107i_is_current_or_former_member_of). The top artists by work count are: Ferdinand Hodler (28), Franz Hegi (26), Alfred Dumont (26), Stéphane Brunner (25), Jakob Samuel Weibel (17), Théophile Alexandre Steinlen (16), Jean-Antoine Linck (16), Jean-Étienne Liotard (12), Wolfgang Adam Toepffer (11), Marc Proessel (9), Martin Disler (9), Barthélemy Bodmer (9), Gérard de Palézieux (9), Davide Antonio Fossati (9). A correct answer should mention at least several of these Swiss artists and some of their works.

question: Which paintings depict Geneva?
sparql_queries:
PREFIX crm: <http://www.cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT ?obj) as ?count)
WHERE {
  ?obj crm:P62_depicts ?depicted .
  ?depicted rdfs:label ?depictedLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?depictedLabel), 'genève') || CONTAINS(LCASE(?depictedLabel), 'geneva'))
}

PREFIX crm: <http://www.cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT ?obj) as ?count)
WHERE {
  ?obj crm:P62_depicts ?depicted .
  ?depicted rdfs:label ?depictedLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?depictedLabel), 'genève') || CONTAINS(LCASE(?depictedLabel), 'geneva'))
  ?obj crm:P2_has_type ?type .
  ?type rdfs:label ?typeLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?typeLabel), 'peinture') || CONTAINS(LCASE(?typeLabel), 'painting') || CONTAINS(LCASE(?typeLabel), 'tableau'))
}
reference_answer: There are 462 works that depict Geneva (entities with 'Genève' or 'Geneva' in their label via P62_depicts). When restricted to works typed as 'peinture' (painting), the count is 205 paintings. The depicted entity is primarily the place Genève. Works include landscapes, cityscapes, and scenes of Geneva and its surroundings. A correct answer should acknowledge the large number and list some representative paintings.

Enfant nu debout vu de face

Trois femmes drapées vues de face

sparql_queries:
PREFIX crm: <http://www.cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT ?birthDate ?birthPlace ?deathDate ?deathPlace
WHERE {
  <https://data.mahmah.ch/agent/8679> crm:P92i_was_brought_into_existence_by ?birth .
  ?birth crm:P4_has_time-span ?bts . ?bts crm:P81a_end_of_the_begin ?birthDate .
  ?birth crm:P7_took_place_at ?bp . ?bp rdfs:label ?birthPlace .
  <https://data.mahmah.ch/agent/8679> crm:P93i_was_taken_out_of_existence_by ?death .
  ?death crm:P4_has_time-span ?dts . ?dts crm:P81a_end_of_the_begin ?deathDate .
  ?death crm:P7_took_place_at ?dp . ?dp rdfs:label ?deathPlace .
} LIMIT 1

PREFIX crm: <http://www.cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?obj ?objLabel
WHERE {
  ?obj crm:P108i_was_produced_by ?prod .
  ?prod crm:P14_carried_out_by <https://data.mahmah.ch/agent/8679> .
  ?obj rdfs:label ?objLabel .
}
ORDER BY ?objLabel
reference_answer: Ferdinand Hodler (born 14 March 1853 in Bern, died 19 May 1918 in Geneva) was a Swiss artist, member of the École suisse. The museum holds 28 works by Hodler, mostly drawings and studies including: figure studies (draped women, nude figures), studies for major compositions (La Bataille de Morat, L'Amour, L'Heure sacrée), portraits (Tête de femme series), and the work titled 'Ferdinand Hodler'. A correct answer should mention his birth/death dates and locations, his membership in the Swiss school, and list several of his key works in the museum collection.

sparql_queries:
PREFIX crm: <http://www.cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?exhibition ?exhibLabel
WHERE {
  ?obj crm:P108i_was_produced_by ?prod .
  ?prod crm:P14_carried_out_by <https://data.mahmah.ch/agent/8679> .
  ?obj crm:P16i_was_used_for ?exhibition .
  ?exhibition rdfs:label ?exhibLabel .
}
ORDER BY ?exhibLabel

PREFIX crm: <http://www.cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?exhibition ?exhibLabel
WHERE {
  ?exhibition a crm:E7_Activity .
  ?exhibition rdfs:label ?exhibLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?exhibLabel), 'hodler'))
}
ORDER BY ?exhibLabel
reference_answer: There are 81 exhibitions with 'Hodler' in their name that contain works from the museum collection (via P16i_was_used_for links). Additionally, 2 exhibitions link directly to Hodler's works without having his name in the title (e.g., 'Carte blanche à Ugo Rondinone'). Notable exhibitions include: 'Hodler // Parallélisme', 'Ferdinand Hodler et Genève', 'Ferdinand Hodler-Fotoalbum', 'Ferdinand Hodler, rétrospective', 'Hodler. Oeuvres sur papier', 'Ferdinand Hodler. Le Paysage', 'Dessins genevois de Liotard à Hodler', and many more. A correct answer should list several key exhibitions.

question: Are there any articles talking about the exhibitions of Hodler?

key_entities.label: L'exposition Hodler à la Galerie Moos

sparql_queries:
PREFIX crm: <http://www.cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?item ?itemLabel
WHERE {
  ?digital crm:P67_refers_to <https://data.mahmah.ch/agent/8679> .
  ?item crm:P128_carries ?digital .
  ?item rdfs:label ?itemLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?itemLabel), 'hodler') && CONTAINS(LCASE(?itemLabel), 'exposition'))
}
ORDER BY ?itemLabel

PREFIX crm: <http://www.cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT DISTINCT ?item ?itemLabel
WHERE {
  ?digital crm:P67_refers_to <https://data.mahmah.ch/agent/8679> .
  ?item crm:P128_carries ?digital .
  ?item rdfs:label ?itemLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?itemLabel), 'exposition'))
}
ORDER BY ?itemLabel

PREFIX crm: <http://www.cidoc-crm.org/cidoc-crm/>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>

SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT ?doc) as ?count)
WHERE {
  ?doc a crm:E31_Document .
  ?doc rdfs:label ?docLabel .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(?docLabel), 'hodler') && CONTAINS(LCASE(?docLabel), 'exposition'))
}
reference_answer: Yes. There are articles (press clippings from the archive) that discuss Hodler exhibitions. Through the P67_refers_to chain (archive item -> P128_carries -> digital item -> P67_refers_to -> Hodler agent), 3 articles specifically mention both 'Hodler' and 'exposition' in their title: 'L'exposition Hodler à Bruxelles', 'L'exposition Hodler à la Galerie Moos', and 'Une exposition Hodler'. Additionally, there are 29 articles in broader P67-linked results mentioning 'exposition'. The E31_Document catalog also has 29 documents with both 'hodler' and 'exposition' in their labels, but these are mostly exhibition catalogues rather than articles. A correct answer should mention at least the 3 key archive articles.

question: Tell me about the article "Une exposition Hodler".

key_entities.label: Coupures de presse de "La Suisse" des années 1920

reference_answer: "Une exposition Hodler" is an E22_Human-Made_Object (archive item, URI: archive/item/2198481). It is a 'Coupure de presse' (press clipping) of type matching AAT 300026030. Its description is: 'Exposition Ferdinand Hodler au Musée d'art et d'histoire'. It was created (P108i_was_produced_by) by Lucienne Florentin (agent/58395) on 1921-07-12 (July 12, 1921). It forms part of (P46i_forms_part_of) the dossier 'Coupures de presse de "La Suisse" des années 1920'. It carries a digital representation (P128_carries) and refers to Ferdinand Hodler via the P67 chain. A correct answer should mention: the title, that it is a press clipping about a Hodler exhibition at the MAH, written by Lucienne Florentin, dated July 12, 1921, and part of a collection of press clippings from the newspaper La Suisse from the 1920s.
